title: Parambikulam Tiger Reserve
tagline: Parambikulam Tiger Reserve — wildlife reserve of Kerala

overview.short: Parambikulam Tiger Reserve, which also includes the erstwhile Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, is a 643.66 square kilometres (248.5 mi2) protected area lying in Palakkad district a
overview.description: Parambikulam Tiger Reserve, which also includes the erstwhile Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, is a 643.66 square kilometres (248.5 mi2) protected area lying in Palakkad district and Thrissur district of Kerala state, South India. The Wildlife Sanctuary, which had an area of 285 square kilometres (110 mi2) was established in part in 1973 and 1984. It is in the Sungam range of hills between the Anaimalai Hills and Nelliampathy Hills. Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary was declared as part of the Parambikulam Tiger Reserve on 19 February 2010.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Karimala Gopuram
topPlaces.description: Karimala Gopuram (കരിമല ഗോപുരം) is the highest peak in the Thrissur district of Kerala. It is located at southern boundary of Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Parambikulam Dam
topPlaces.description: Parambikulam Dam is an embankment dam on the Parambikulam River, located in the Palakkad district in the Western Ghats of Kerala, India, ranks number one in India as well as in the top ten embankment dams in the world in volume in the year 2000. This Dam was built at the time of Kamarajar.

topPlaces.name: Kannimara Teak
topPlaces.description: The Kannimara Teak is one of the world's biggest living teak (Tectona grandis) trees. It is found at Parambikulam Tiger Reserve in Palakkad district, Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Sholayar Dam
topPlaces.description: Sholayar Dam is a concrete dam built across the Chalakkudi River in Malakkappara in Thrissur district, Kerala of India. The dam consists of main Sholayar Dam, Sholayar Flanking and Sholayar Saddle Dam.

topPlaces.name: Solaiyar Dam
topPlaces.description: Solaiyar Dam, Upper Solaiyar Dam or Upper Sholayar Dam (Solaiyaaru) is located 20 km (12 mi) from Valparai, a hill station in the Anaimalai Hills of the Coimbatore district, Tamil Nadu India. As it is a part of the hydroelectric project of Tamil Nadu, special permission is required to visit the dam.

topPlaces.name: Peruvaripallam Dam
topPlaces.description: Peruvaripallam Dam is an earth-filled embankment dam on the Peruvaripallam River in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. The dam's reservoir is connected to the nearby Thunacadavu Reservoir to the south by an open cut channel.

topPlaces.name: Thunakkadavu Dam
topPlaces.description: Thunakkadavu Dam is situated in Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, Kerala across Thoonakkadavu River, which is a tributary of Parambikulam River, in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. It is part of the Parambikulam-Aliyar Irrigation Project.

items.note: Karimala Gopuram (കരിമല ഗോപുരം) is the highest peak in the Thrissur district of Kerala. It is located at southern boundary of Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary.

Parambikulam Dam is an embankment dam on the Parambikulam River, located in the Palakkad district in the Western Ghats of Kerala, India, ranks number one in India as well as in the top ten embankment dams in the world in volume in the year 2000. This Dam was built at the time of Kamarajar.
The Kannimara Teak is one of the world's biggest living teak (Tectona grandis) trees. It is found at Parambikulam Tiger Reserve in Palakkad district, Kerala.

Sholayar Dam is a concrete dam built across the Chalakkudi River in Malakkappara in Thrissur district, Kerala of India. The dam consists of main Sholayar Dam, Sholayar Flanking and Sholayar Saddle Dam.

Solaiyar Dam, Upper Solaiyar Dam or Upper Sholayar Dam (Solaiyaaru) is located 20 km (12 mi) from Valparai, a hill station in the Anaimalai Hills of the Coimbatore district, Tamil Nadu India. As it is a part of the hydroelectric project of Tamil Nadu, special permission is required to visit the dam.

Peruvaripallam Dam is an earth-filled embankment dam on the Peruvaripallam River in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. The dam's reservoir is connected to the nearby Thunacadavu Reservoir to the south by an open cut channel.

Thunakkadavu Dam is situated in Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, Kerala across Thoonakkadavu River, which is a tributary of Parambikulam River, in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. It is part of the Parambikulam-Aliyar Irrigation Project.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Parambikulam Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Parambikulam Tiger Reserve is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Parambikulam Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (42 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (65 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Parambikulam Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Parambikulam Tiger Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Parambikulam Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Parambikulam Tiger Reserve start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Parambikulam Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karimala Gopuram, Parambikulam Dam, Kannimara Teak, Sholayar Dam, Solaiyar Dam.
